1.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ سُورَةٌ أَنزَلْنَـٰهَا وَفَرَضْنَـٰهَا وَأَنزَلْنَا فِيهَآ ءَايَـٰتٍۭ بَيِّنَـٰتٍ لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: WE HAVE REVEALED this Surah and made it obligatory as We have sent down clear injunctions in it that you may be warned.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is a chapter which We have sent down and decreed its commands, and in which We have sent down clear verses, in order that you may ponder.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: A sura that We have sent down and appointed; and We have sent down in it signs, clear signs, that haply you will remember.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This is a chapter which We have sent down, and which We have ordained; and therein We have sent down revelations manifest, that haply ye may be admonished.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (This is) a Surah (chapter of the Quran) which We have sent down and which We have enjoined, (ordained its legal laws) and in it We have revealed manifest Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations lawful and unlawful things, and set boundries of Islamic Religion), that you may remember.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: A chapter that We have revealed, and made obligatory, and revealed in it clear Verses, that you may take heed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is a Surah which We have sent down and We have made it mandatory, and We have sent down clear Commandments in it so that you may learn lessons.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: A Surah which We have sent down and which We have enjoined (Faradnaha), and in it We have revealed manifest Ayat, that you may remember.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (Here is) a surah which We have revealed and enjoined, and wherein We have revealed plain tokens, that haply ye may take heed.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: [This is] a surah which We have sent down and prescribed, and We have sent down in it manifest signs so that you may take admonition.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: This is a chapter which We have sent down and appointed; and in it We have sent down clear verses in order that you will remember.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [This is] a surah which We have sent down and made [that within it] obligatory and revealed therein verses of clear evidence that you might remember.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is a chapter which We have revealed to you and made obligatory for you to follow its guidance. We have revealed clear verses in it so that perhaps you may take heed.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: (This is) a chapter which We have revealed and made obligatory and in which We have revealed clear communications that you may be mindful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: This is a chapter which We have revealed and which We have made obligatory; We have sent down clear revelations in it, so that you may take heed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: A sura which We have sent down and which We have ordained in it have We sent down Clear Signs, in order that ye may receive admonition.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Sooratun anzalnaha wafaradnaha waanzalna feeha ayatin bayyinatin laAAallakum tathakkaroona

2.arabic: ٱلزَّانِيَةُ وَٱلزَّانِى فَٱجْلِدُوا۟ كُلَّ وَٰحِدٍ مِّنْهُمَا مِا۟ئَةَ جَلْدَةٍ ۖ وَلَا تَأْخُذْكُم بِهِمَا رَأْفَةٌ فِى دِينِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۖ وَلْيَشْهَدْ عَذَابَهُمَا طَآئِفَةٌ مِّنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The adulteress and adulterer should be flogged a hundred lashes each, and no pity for them should deter you from the law of God, if you believe in God and the Last Day; and the punishment should be witnessed by a body of believers.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The adulteress and the adulterer – punish each one of them with a hundred lashes; and may you not have pity on them in the religion to Allah, if you believe in Allah and the Last Day; and a group of believers must witness their punishment.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: The fornicatress and the fornicator -- scourge each one of them a hundred stripes, and in the matter of God's religion let no tenderness for them seize you if you believe in God and the Last Day; and let a party of the believers witness their chastisement.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The adulteress and the adulterer: scourge each one of the twain with a hundred stripes. And let not tenderness rake hold of you in regarad to the twain in the law of Allah, if ye have come to believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let witness this torment a band of the believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The woman and the man guilty of illegal sexual intercourse, flog each of them with a hundred stripes. Let not pity withhold you in their case, in a punishment prescribed by Allah, if you believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let a party of the believers witness their punishment. (This punishment is for unmarried persons guilty of the above crime but if married persons commit it, the punishment is to stone them to death, according to Allah's Law).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The adulteress and the adulterer—whip each one of them a hundred lashes, and let no pity towards them overcome you regarding God’s Law, if you believe in God and the Last Day. And let a group of believers witness their punishment.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The woman and the man guilty of fornication, flog each one of them with a hundred stripes - and let not any pity for them restrain you in regard to a matter prescribed by Allah, if you believe in Allah and the Last Day, and let, some of the believers witness the punishment inflicted on them.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The Zaniyah and the Zani, flog each of them with a hundred stripes. Let not pity withhold you in their case, in a punishment prescribed by Allah, if you believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let a party of the believers witness their punishment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The adulterer and the adulteress, scourge ye each one of them (with) a hundred stripes. And let not pity for the twain withhold you from obedience to Allah, if ye believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let a party of believers witness their punishment.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for the fornicatress and the fornicator, strike each of them a hundred lashes, and let not pity for them overcome you in Allah’s law, if you believe in Allah and the Last Day, and let their punishment be witnessed by a group of the faithful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: You shall lash the fornicatress and the fornicator each with a hundred lashes. In the religion of Allah, let no tenderness for them seize you if you believe in Allah and the Last Day; and let their punishment be witnessed by a party of believers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The [unmarried] woman or [unmarried] man found guilty of sexual intercourse - lash each one of them with a hundred lashes, and do not be taken by pity for them in the religion of Allah, if you should believe in Allah and the Last Day. And let a group of the believers witness their punishment.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Flog the fornicatress and the fornicator with a hundred lashes each. Let there be no reluctance in enforcing the laws of God, if you have faith in God and the Day of Judgment. Let it take place in the presence of a group of believers.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: (As for) the fornicatress and the fornicator, flog each of them, (giving) a hundred stripes, and let not pity for them detain you in the matter of obedience to Allah, if you believe in Allah and the last day, and let a party of believers witness their chastisement.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Flog the adulteress and the adulterer, each one of them, with a hundred lashes. Let no pity for them cause you to disobey God, if you truly believe in God and the Last Day; and let their punishment be witnessed by a number of believers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The woman and the man guilty of adultery or fornication,- flog each of them with a hundred stripes: Let not compassion move you in their case, in a matter prescribed by Allah, if ye believe in Allah and the Last Day: and let a party of the Believers witness their punishment.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alzzaniyatu waalzzanee faijlidoo kulla wahidin minhuma miata jaldatin wala takhuthkum bihima rafatun fee deeni Allahi in kuntum tuminoona biAllahi waalyawmi alakhiri walyashhad AAathabahuma taifatun mina almumineena

3.arabic: ٱلزَّانِى لَا يَنكِحُ إِلَّا زَانِيَةً أَوْ مُشْرِكَةً وَٱلزَّانِيَةُ لَا يَنكِحُهَآ إِلَّا زَانٍ أَوْ مُشْرِكٌ ۚ وَحُرِّمَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The adulterer can marry no one but an adulteress or his partner (in the act), and the adulteress cannot marry any but an adulterer or her partner (in the act). This is forbidden the believers,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The adulterer shall not marry except an adulteress or a polytheist woman, and none shall marry an adulteress except an adulterer or a polytheist; and this is forbidden for the believers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: The fornicator shall marry none but a fornicatress or an idolatress, and the fornicatress -- none shall marry her but a fornicator or an idolator; that is forbidden to the believers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The adulterer weddeth not but an adulteress or an associatoress: and the adulteress! --none weddeth her save an adulterer or an associator; and that is forbidden unto the believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The adulterer marries not but an adulteress or a Mushrikah and the adulteress none marries her except an adulterer or a Muskrik [and that means that the man who agrees to marry (have a sexual relation with) a Mushrikah (female polytheist, pagan or idolatress) or a prostitute, then surely he is either an adulterer, or a Mushrik (polytheist, pagan or idolater, etc.) And the woman who agrees to marry (have a sexual relation with) a Mushrik (polytheist, pagan or idolater) or an adulterer, then she is either a prostitute or a Mushrikah (female polytheist, pagan, or idolatress, etc.)]. Such a thing is forbidden to the believers (of Islamic Monotheism).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The adulterer shall marry none but an adulteress or an idolatress; and the adulteress shall marry none but an adulterer or an idolater. That has been prohibited for the believers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: A man guilty of adultery (or fornication) shall not marry any but the woman guilty of the same or a mushrik woman, and none shall marry a woman guilty of adultery (or fornication) but the man guilty of the same or a mushrik man: such marriages are forbidden to true believers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The Zani marries not but a Zaniyah or a Mushrikah; and the Zaniyah, none marries her except a Zani or a Mushrik. Such a thing is forbidden to the believers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The adulterer shall not marry save an adulteress or an idolatress, and the adulteress none shall marry save an adulterer or an idolater. All that is forbidden unto believers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The fornicator will not marry anyone but a fornicatress or an idolatress, and the fornicatress will be married by none except a fornicator or an idolater, and that is forbidden to the faithful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The fornicator shall marry none but a fornicatress or an idolatress; and the fornicatress none shall marry her but a fornicator or an idolater; that is forbidden to the believers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The fornicator does not marry except a [female] fornicator or polytheist, and none marries her except a fornicator or a polytheist, and that has been made unlawful to the believers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: No one should marry a fornicator except a fornicatress or a pagan woman. No one should marry a fornicatress except a fornicator or a pagan man. Such (marriage) is unlawful to the believers.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The fornicator shall not marry any but a fornicatress or idolatress, and (as for) the fornicatress, none shall marry her but a fornicator or an idolater; and it is forbidden to the believers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The adulterer shall marry only an adulteress or a polytheist woman, and an adulteress shall marry only an adulterer or a polytheist man; such marriages are forbidden for believers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Let no man guilty of adultery or fornication marry and but a woman similarly guilty, or an Unbeliever: nor let any but such a man or an Unbeliever marry such a woman: to the Believers such a thing is forbidden.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alzzanee la yankihu illa zaniyatan aw mushrikatan waalzzaniyatu la yankihuha illa zanin aw mushrikun wahurrima thalika AAala almumineena

4.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ ٱلْمُحْصَنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَأْتُوا۟ بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَآءَ فَٱجْلِدُوهُمْ ثَمَـٰنِينَ جَلْدَةً وَلَا تَقْبَلُوا۟ لَهُمْ شَهَـٰدَةً أَبَدًا ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who defame chaste women and do not bring four witnesses should be punished with eighty lashes, and their testimony should not be accepted afterwards, for they are profligates,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And those who accuse chaste women and do not bring four witnesses to testify – punish them with eighty lashes and do not ever accept their testimony; and it is they who are the wicked.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And those who cast it up on women in wedlock, and then bring not four witnesses, scourge them with eighty stripes, and do not accept any testimony of theirs ever; those -- they are the ungodly,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And those who accuse clean wowomen and then bring not four eyewitnesses, Scourge them with eighty stripes and accept not their- testimony for Ever. And these! they are the transgressors

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And those who accuse chaste women, and produce not four witnesses, flog them with eighty stripes, and reject their testimony forever, they indeed are the Fasiqun (liars, rebellious, disobedient to Allah).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who accuse chaste women, then cannot bring four witnesses, whip them eighty lashes, and do not ever accept their testimony. For these are the immoral.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for those persons who charge chaste women with false accusations but do not produce four witnesses, flog them with eighty stripes and never accept their evidence afterwards, for they themselves are transgressors,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And those who accuse chaste women, and produce not four witnesses, flog them with eighty stripes, and reject their testimony forever. They indeed are the rebellious.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And those who accuse honourable women but bring not four witnesses, scourge them (with) eighty stripes and never (afterward) accept their testimony - They indeed are evil-doers -

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for those who accuse chaste women and do not bring four witnesses, strike them eighty lashes, and never accept any testimony from them after that, and they are transgressors,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who accuse chaste women, and cannot produce four witnesses, you shall lash them with eighty lashes. And never accept their testimony, for they are evildoers,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And those who accuse chaste women and then do not produce four witnesses - lash them with eighty lashes and do not accept from them testimony ever after. And those are the defiantly disobedient,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who accuse married women of committing adultery - but are not able to prove their accusation by producing four witness - must be flogged eighty lashes. Never accept their testimony thereafter because they are sinful,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And those who accuse free women then do not bring four witnesses, flog them, (giving) eighty stripes, and do not admit any evidence from them ever; and these it is that are the transgressors,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who defame chaste women, but cannot produce four witnesses, shall be given eighty lashes. Do not accept their testimony ever after, for they are transgressors,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And those who launch a charge against chaste women, and produce not four witnesses (to support their allegations),- flog them with eighty stripes; and reject their evidence ever after: for such men are wicked transgressors;-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waallatheena yarmoona almuhsanati thumma lam yatoo biarbaAAati shuhadaa faijlidoohum thamaneena jaldatan wala taqbaloo lahum shahadatan abadan waolaika humu alfasiqoona

5.arabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ تَابُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Except those who repent after this and reform; and God is surely forgiving and kind.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Except those who repent after this and reform themselves; so indeed Allah is Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: save such as repent thereafter and make amends; surely God is All-forgiving, All-compassionate.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Excepting those who thereafter shall repent and make amends. Verily Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Except those who repent thereafter and do righteous deeds, (for such) verily, Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Except for those who repent afterwards, and reform; for God is Forgiving and Merciful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: except those who repent and reform themselves; Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Except those who repent thereafter and do righteous deeds; (for such) verily, Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Save those who afterward repent and make amends. (For such) lo! Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: excepting those who repent after that and reform, for Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: except those among them that afterwards repent and mend their ways. Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Except for those who repent thereafter and reform, for indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: except that of those who afterwards repent and reform themselves; God is All-forgiving and All-merciful.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Except those who repent after this and act aright, for surely Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: save those who afterwards repent and make amends, for truly God is forgiving and merciful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Unless they repent thereafter and mend (their conduct); for Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Illa allatheena taboo min baAAdi thalika waaslahoo fainna Allaha ghafoorun raheemun

6.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ أَزْوَٰجَهُمْ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُمْ شُهَدَآءُ إِلَّآ أَنفُسُهُمْ فَشَهَـٰدَةُ أَحَدِهِمْ أَرْبَعُ شَهَـٰدَٰتٍۭ بِٱللَّهِ ۙ إِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who accuse their wives and do not have any witnesses except themselves, should swear four times in the name of God, the testimony of each such person being that he is speaking the truth,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And those who accuse their wives and do not have witnesses except their own statements – for such the testimony is that he bear the testimony four times by the name of Allah that he is truthful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And those who cast it up on their wives having no witnesses except themselves, the testimony of one of them shall be to testify by God four times that he is of the truthful,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And as for those who accuse their wives and there are not for them witnesses except themselves, the testimony of one of them shall be four testimonies by Allah: that verily he is of the truth-tellers

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And for those who accuse their wives, but have no witnesses except themselves, let the testimony of one of them be four testimonies (i.e. testifies four times) by Allah that he is one of those who speak the truth.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As for those who accuse their own spouses, but have no witnesses except themselves, the testimony of one of them is equivalent to four testimonies, if he swears by God that he is truthful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for those who accuse their own wives but have no witness except themselves, the evidence of one of them is that he shall swear four times by Allah and declare that he is true (in his charge).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And for those who accuse their wives, but have no witnesses except themselves, let the testimony of one of them be four testimonies by Allah that he is one of those who speak the truth.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: As for those who accuse their wives but have no witnesses except themselves; let the testimony of one of them be four testimonies, (swearing) by Allah that he is of those who speak the truth;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for those who accuse their wives [of adultery], but have no witnesses except themselves, the testimony of such a man shall be a fourfold testimony [sworn] by Allah that he is indeed stating the truth,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And those who accuse their wives and have no witnesses except themselves, let them testify by swearing by Allah four times that he is of the truthful,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And those who accuse their wives [of adultery] and have no witnesses except themselves - then the witness of one of them [shall be] four testimonies [swearing] by Allah that indeed, he is of the truthful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who accuse their spouses of committing adultery but have no witness except themselves, should testify four times saying, "God is my witness that I am telling the truth".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And (as for) those who accuse their wives and have no witnesses except themselves, the evidence of one of these (should be taken) four times, bearing Allah to witness that he is most surely of the truthful ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: One who accuses his wife and has no witnesses except himself shall swear four times by God that his charge is true,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And for those who launch a charge against their spouses, and have (in support) no evidence but their own,- their solitary evidence (can be received) if they bear witness four times (with an oath) by Allah that they are solemnly telling the truth;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waallatheena yarmoona azwajahum walam yakun lahum shuhadao illa anfusuhum fashahadatu ahadihim arbaAAu shahadatin biAllahi innahu lamina alssadiqeena

7.arabic: وَٱلْخَـٰمِسَةُ أَنَّ لَعْنَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ إِن كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And (swear) a fifth time that if he tell a lie the curse of God be on him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the fifth time, that the curse of Allah be upon him if he is a liar.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and a fifth time, that the curse of God shall be upon him, if he should be of the liars.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And the fifth, that the curse of Allah be upon him if he be of the liars.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And the fifth (testimony) (should be) the invoking of the Curse of Allah on him if he be of those who tell a lie (against her).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And the fifth time, that God’s curse be upon him, if he is a liar.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then the fifth time he shall declare that Allah's curse be upon him if he be false (in his charge).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And the fifth (testimony); invoking of the curse of Allah on him if he be of those who tell a lie.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And yet a fifth, invoking the curse of Allah on him if he is of those who lie.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and a fifth [oath] that Allah’s wrath shall be upon him if he were lying.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and the fifth time, that the curse of Allah shall be upon him, if he should be of the liars.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And the fifth [oath will be] that the curse of Allah be upon him if he should be among the liars.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They should say on the fifth time, "Let God's curse be upon me if I am a liar".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And the fifth (time) that the curse of Allah be on him if he is one of the liars.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and the fifth time, that God's curse may be upon him if he is telling a lie.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And the fifth (oath) (should be) that they solemnly invoke the curse of Allah on themselves if they tell a lie.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waalkhamisatu anna laAAnata Allahi AAalayhi in kana mina alkathibeena

8.arabic: وَيَدْرَؤُا۟ عَنْهَا ٱلْعَذَابَ أَن تَشْهَدَ أَرْبَعَ شَهَـٰدَٰتٍۭ بِٱللَّهِ ۙ إِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The woman's punishment can be averted if she swears four times by God as testimony that her husband is a liar,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the punishment shall be averted from the woman if she bears the testimony four times by the name of Allah, that the man is a liar.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: It shall avert from her the chastisement if she testify by God four times that he is of the liars,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And it will revert the chastisement from her if she testifieth by Allah four times that verily he is of the liars.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But it shall avert the punishment (of stoning to death) from her, if she bears witness four times by Allah, that he (her husband) is telling a lie.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But punishment shall be averted from her, if she swears four times by God, that he is a liar.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (As for the woman), it shall avert the punishment from her if she swears four times by Allah that the man is false (in his charge)

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But she shall avert the punishment from her, if she bears witness four times by Allah, that he is telling a lie.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And it shall avert the punishment from her if she bear witness before Allah four times that the thing he saith is indeed false,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The punishment shall be averted from her by her testifying with four oaths [sworn] by Allah that he is indeed lying,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But the punishment will be averted from her if she swears four times that he is of the liars,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But it will prevent punishment from her if she gives four testimonies [swearing] by Allah that indeed, he is of the liars.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The spouse will be acquitted of the punishment if she challenges his testimony by saying four times, "God is my witness that he is a liar".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And it shall avert the chastisement from her if she testify four times, bearing Allah to witness that he is most surely one of the liars;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The wife shall receive no punishment, if she bears witness four times in the name of God that her husband has lied

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But it would avert the punishment from the wife, if she bears witness four times (with an oath) By Allah, that (her husband) is telling a lie;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wayadrao AAanha alAAathaba an tashhada arbaAAa shahadatin biAllahi innahu lamina alkathibeena

9.arabic: وَٱلْخَـٰمِسَةَ أَنَّ غَضَبَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْهَآ إِن كَانَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Her fifth oath being that the curse of God be on her if her husband should be speaking the truth.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the fifth time, that the wrath of Allah be upon her if the man is truthful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and a fifth time, that the wrath of God shall be upon her, if he should be of the truthful.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And the fifth that Allah's wrath be upon her if he is of the truthtellers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And the fifth (testimony) should be that the Wrath of Allah be upon her if he (her husband) speaks the truth.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And the fifth time, that God’s wrath be upon her, if he is truthful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and the fifth time she invokes Allah's wrath upon herself, if he be true (in his charge).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And the fifth; should be that the wrath of Allah be upon her if he speaks the truth.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And a fifth (time) that the wrath of Allah be upon her if he speaketh truth.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and a fifth [oath] that Allah’s wrath shall be upon her if he were stating the truth.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and on the fifth time the Wrath of Allah shall be upon her if he is of the truthful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And the fifth [oath will be] that the wrath of Allah be upon her if he was of the truthful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: On the fifth time she should say, "Let the curse of God be upon me if what he says is true."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And the fifth (time) that the wrath of Allah be on her if he is one of the truthful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and, a fifth time that God's wrath will be upon her if he is telling the truth.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And the fifth (oath) should be that she solemnly invokes the wrath of Allah on herself if (her accuser) is telling the truth.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waalkhamisata anna ghadaba Allahi AAalayha in kana mina alssadiqeena

10.arabic: وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ تَوَّابٌ حَكِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: (This would not have been possible) if the grace and benevolence of God were not upon you; but God is compassionate and wise.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And were it not for Allah’s munificence and His mercy upon you and that Allah is the Acceptor of Repentance, the Wise – He would then have unveiled you.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But for God's bounty to you and His mercy and that God turns, and is All-wise --

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had it not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy unto you and that Allah is Relenting, Wise, ye had been lost.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And had it not been for the Grace of Allah and His Mercy on you (He would have hastened the punishment upon you)! And that Allah is the One Who accepts repentance, the All-Wise.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Were it not for God’s grace upon you, and His mercy, and that God is Conciliatory and Wise.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: If Allah had not shown you His grace and mercy and if Allah had not been most Forgiving and All-Wise, (you would have been in a great fix because of accusing your wives).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And had it not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy on you! And that Allah is the One Who forgives and accepts repentance, the All-Wise.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And had it not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy unto you, and that Allah is Clement, Wise, (ye had been undone).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you, and that Allah is all-clement, all-wise....

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If it were not for the bounty of Allah to you and His Mercy, and that Allah turns, and is the Wise,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if not for the favor of Allah upon you and His mercy... and because Allah is Accepting of repentance and Wise.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Had it not been for God's favors and mercy upon you (your life would have been in chaos). God accepts repentance and He is All-wise.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And were it not for Allah's grace upon you and His mercy-- and that Allah is Oft-returning (to mercy), Wise!

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Were it not for God's grace and His mercy upon you, [you would have come to grief] and God is wise, acceptor of repentance.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If it were not for Allah's grace and mercy on you, and that Allah is Oft-Returning, full of Wisdom,- (Ye would be ruined indeed).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walawla fadlu Allahi AAalaykum warahmatuhu waanna Allaha tawwabun hakeemun

11.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ جَآءُو بِٱلْإِفْكِ عُصْبَةٌ مِّنكُمْ ۚ لَا تَحْسَبُوهُ شَرًّا لَّكُم ۖ بَلْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۚ لِكُلِّ ٱمْرِئٍ مِّنْهُم مَّا ٱكْتَسَبَ مِنَ ٱلْإِثْمِ ۚ وَٱلَّذِى تَوَلَّىٰ كِبْرَهُۥ مِنْهُمْ لَهُۥ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who spread lies were a clique among you. Do not think that it was bad for you: In fact it has been good for you. Each of them will pay for the sin he has committed, and he who had greater share (of guilt) will suffer grievous punishment.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed those who have brought this great accusation are a group from among you; do not consider it bad for you; on the contrary, it is good for you; for each man among them is the sin that he has earned; and for the one among them who played the greatest part in it – for him is a terrible punishment.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those who came with the slander are a band of you; do not reckon it evil for you; rather it is good for you. Every man of them shall have the sin that he has earned charged to him; and whosoever of them took upon himself the greater part of it, him there awaits a mighty chastisement.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who brought forward the calumny were a small band among Deem it not an evil for you; nay, it was good for you. Unto every one of them shall be that which he hath earned of the sin, and he among them who undertook the bulk of it---for him shall be a torment mighty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! Those who brought forth the slander (against 'Aishah, the wife of the Prophet SAW) are a group among you. Consider it not a bad thing for you. Nay, it is good for you. Unto every man among them will be paid that which he had earned of the sin, and as for him among them who had the greater share therein, his will be a great torment.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who perpetrated the slander are a band of you. Do not consider it bad for you, but it is good for you. Each person among them bears his share in the sin. As for him who played the major role—for him is a terrible punishment.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Those who have invented the slander, are some of your own people. You should not, however, regard this matter as evil for it has good in it for you. Whoso took any part in this, he earned his share of the sin accordingly, and the one, who had the greatest share of responsibility in it, shall have a terrible punishment.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who brought forth the slander are a group among you. Consider it not a bad thing for you. Nay, it is good for you. Unto every man among them will be paid that which he had earned of the sin, and as for him among them who had the greater share therein, his will be a great torment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! they who spread the slander are a gang among you. Deem it not a bad thing for you; nay, it is good for you. Unto every man of them (will be paid) that which he hath earned of the sin; and as for him among them who had the greater share therein, his will be an awful doom.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed those who initiated the calumny are a group from among yourselves. Do not suppose it is bad thing for you. No, it is for your good. Each man among them bears [the onus for] his share in the sin, and as for him who assumed its major burden from among them, there is a great punishment for him.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: those who came with the slander were a number of you. Do not regard it evil for you, rather it is good for you. Every person of them shall have the sin that he has earned charged to him. As for he who took upon himself the greater part there is a mightier punishment.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who came with falsehood are a group among you. Do not think it bad for you; rather it is good for you. For every person among them is what [punishment] he has earned from the sin, and he who took upon himself the greater portion thereof - for him is a great punishment.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those of you who have brought forward a false report (against Aisha) do not think that it will harm you. Rather it will be a benefit to you. Each one of you will face the result of his sin. The one among the group who was the chief instigator will face a great torment.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely they who concocted the lie are a party from among you. Do not regard it an evil to you; nay, it is good for you. Every man of them shall have what he has earned of sin; and (as for) him who took upon himself the main part thereof, he shall have a grievous chastisement.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who brought up that slander were a band from among you. Do not regard it as a misfortune, for it is good for you. Every one of them shall be held to account for the sin he has committed; and he who took the greater part in it shall have a terrible punishment.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who brought forward the lie are a body among yourselves: think it not to be an evil to you; On the contrary it is good for you: to every man among them (will come the punishment) of the sin that he earned, and to him who took on himself the lead among them, will be a penalty grievous.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena jaoo bialifki AAusbatun minkum la tahsaboohu sharran lakum bal huwa khayrun lakum likulli imriin minhum ma iktasaba mina alithmi waallathee tawalla kibrahu minhum lahu AAathabun AAatheemun

12.arabic: لَّوْلَآ إِذْ سَمِعْتُمُوهُ ظَنَّ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتُ بِأَنفُسِهِمْ خَيْرًا وَقَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَآ إِفْكٌ مُّبِينٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Why did the faithful men and women not think well of their people when they heard this, and said: "This is a clear lie?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Why was it not that the believing men and women, when you heard it, thought good of their own people, and had said, “This is a clear accusation”?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Why, when you heard it, did the believing men and women not of their own account think good thoughts, and say, 'This is a manifest calumny'?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore, when ye heard it, did not the believing men and believing women imagine best of their own people and say: this is a calumny manifest!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Why then, did not the believers, men and women, when you heard it (the slander) think good of their own people and say: "This (charge) is an obvious lie?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Why, when you heard about it, the believing men and women did not think well of one another, and say, “This is an obvious lie”?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When you heard of it, why didn't the Believing men and the Believing women have a good opinion of themselves, and why did they not say, "This is a manifest slander?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Why then, did not the believers, men and women, when you heard it, think good of their own people and say: "This is an obvious lie"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Why did not the believers, men and women, when ye heard it, think good of their own own folk, and say: It is a manifest untruth?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When you [first] heard about it, why did not the faithful, men and women, think well of their folks, and say, ‘This is an obvious calumny’?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Had you heard it, and the believing men and women, thought good thoughts about one another said: 'This is a clear falsehood'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Why, when you heard it, did not the believing men and believing women think good of one another and say, "This is an obvious falsehood"?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Would that on your hearing this report, the believing men and woman among you had a favorable attitude towards it, and had said, "This report is clearly false".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Why did not the believing men and the believing women, when you heard it, think well of their own people, and say: This is an evident falsehood?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When you heard of it, why did not the believing men and believing women think well of their own people, and say, "This is a manifest slander?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Why did not the believers - men and women - when ye heard of the affair,- put the best construction on it in their own minds and say, "This (charge) is an obvious lie"?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Lawla ith samiAAtumoohu thanna almuminoona waalmuminatu bianfusihim khayran waqaloo hatha ifkun mubeenun

13.arabic: لَّوْلَا جَآءُو عَلَيْهِ بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَآءَ ۚ فَإِذْ لَمْ يَأْتُوا۟ بِٱلشُّهَدَآءِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰذِبُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Why did they not bring four witnesses (in support of their charge)? And since they did not bring the four witnesses they are themselves liars in the sight of God.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Why did they not bring four witnesses upon it? Since they did not bring witnesses, they themselves are liars before Allah.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Why did they not bring four witnesses against it? But since they did not bring the witnesses, in God's sight they are the liars.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore did they not bring four witnesses thereof? Then when they brought not the witnesses, those! with Allah they are the liars.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Why did they not produce four witnesses? Since they (the slanderers) have not produced witnesses! Then with Allah they are the liars.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Why did they not bring four witnesses to testify to it? If they fail to bring the witnesses, then in God’s sight, they are liars.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Why did the slanderers not bring four witnesses (to prove their charge)? Now that they have not brought witnesses, they themselves are liars in the sight of Allah.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Why did they not produce four witnesses against him Since they have not produced witnesses! Then with Allah, they are the liars.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Why did they not produce four witnesses? Since they produce not witnesses, they verily are liars in the sight of Allah.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Why did they not bring four witnesses to it? So when they could not bring the witnesses, they are liars in Allah’s sight.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Why, did they not bring four witnesses against it? But since they did not bring the witnesses, before Allah they are the liars.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Why did they [who slandered] not produce for it four witnesses? And when they do not produce the witnesses, then it is they, in the sight of Allah, who are the liars.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Would that they had brought four witness to testify to their report. Since they brought none, they are liars before God.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Why did they not bring four witnesses of it? But as they have not brought witnesses they are liars before Allah.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Why did they not produce four witnesses? If they could not produce any witnesses, they were indeed liars in the sight of God!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Why did they not bring four witnesses to prove it? When they have not brought the witnesses, such men, in the sight of Allah, (stand forth) themselves as liars!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Lawla jaoo AAalayhi biarbaAAati shuhadaa faith lam yatoo bialshshuhadai faolaika AAinda Allahi humu alkathiboona

14.arabic: وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ لَمَسَّكُمْ فِى مَآ أَفَضْتُمْ فِيهِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Were it not for the grace of God and His mercy upon you in this world and the next, you would have suffered a great affliction for the false accusation.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And were it not for Allah’s munificence and His mercy upon you in the world and in the Hereafter, a terrible punishment would have reached you for the discussions you fell into.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But for God's bounty to you and His mercy in the present world and the world to come there would have visited you for your mutterings a mighty chastisement.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Had there not been Allah's grace upon you and His mercy in the World and the Hereafter, surely there would have touched you for that whcrein ye had rushed, torment mighty--

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Had it not been for the Grace of Allah and His Mercy unto you in this world and in the Hereafter, a great torment would have touched you for that whereof you had spoken.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Were it not for God’s favor upon you, and His mercy, in this world and the Hereafter, you would have suffered a great punishment for what you have ventured into.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Were it not for Allah's grace and mercy towards you in this world and in the Hereafter, a painful scourge would have visited you because of the things in which you were involved.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Had it not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy unto you in this world and in the Hereafter, a great torment would have touched you for that whereof you had spoken.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Had it not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy unto you in the world and the Hereafter an awful doom had overtaken you for that whereof ye murmured.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you in this world and the Hereafter, there would have befallen you a great punishment for what you ventured into,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But for the bounty of Allah and His Mercy towards you in this life and in the Everlasting Life you would have been sternly punished for that which you were involved.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if it had not been for the favor of Allah upon you and His mercy in this world and the Hereafter, you would have been touched for that [lie] in which you were involved by a great punishment

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Were it not for the favors and mercy of God upon you, in this world and in the life to come, a great torment would strike you for your involvement in this false report.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And were it not for Allah's grace upon you and His mercy in this world and the hereafter, a grievous chastisement would certainly have touched you on account of the discourse which you entered into.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Had it not been for the grace of God and His mercy on you in this world and the Hereafter, a terrible punishment would have afflicted you for your plunging headlong into slander.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Were it not for the grace and mercy of Allah on you, in this world and the Hereafter, a grievous penalty would have seized you in that ye rushed glibly into this affair.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walawla fadlu Allahi AAalaykum warahmatuhu fee alddunya waalakhirati lamassakum fee ma afadtum feehi AAathabun AAatheemun

15.arabic: إِذْ تَلَقَّوْنَهُۥ بِأَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَتَقُولُونَ بِأَفْوَاهِكُم مَّا لَيْسَ لَكُم بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ وَتَحْسَبُونَهُۥ هَيِّنًا وَهُوَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ عَظِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When you talked about it and said what you did not know, and took it lightly -- though in the sight of God it was serious --

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When you rumoured with your tongues after hearing such matters, and uttered with your mouths about which you had no knowledge, and you considered it light; and that, in the sight of Allah, is very great.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When you received it on your tongues, and were speaking with your mouths that whereof you had no knowledge, and reckoned it a light thing, and with God it was a mighty thing --

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: When ye were publishing it with your tongues and saying that with your mouths of which ye had no knowledge. Ye deemed it light, and it was with Allah mighty!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: When you were propagating it with your tongues, and uttering with your mouths that whereof you had no knowledge, you counted it a little thing, while with Allah it was very great.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When you rumored it with your tongues, and spoke with your mouths what you had no knowledge of, and you considered it trivial; but according to God, it is serious.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (Just think how erroneous you were,) when you passed this lie on from one tongue to the other and uttered with your mouths that of which you had no knowledge. You took it as a trifling matter whereas it was a grave offence in the sight of Allah.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: When you were propagating it with your tongues, and uttering with your mouths that whereof you had no knowledge, you counted it a little thing, while with Allah it was very great.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When ye welcomed it with your tongues, and uttered with your mouths that whereof ye had no knowledge, ye counted it a trifle. In the sight of Allah it is very great.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: when you were receiving it on your tongues, and were mouthing something of which you had no knowledge, supposing it to be a light matter, while it was a grave [matter] with Allah.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: You carried with your tongues and uttered with your mouths what you did not know. You have thought it a trifle, but before Allah it was a mighty thing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: When you received it with your tongues and said with your mouths that of which you had no knowledge and thought it was insignificant while it was, in the sight of Allah, tremendous.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Your tongues moved and your mouths spoke of something of which you had no knowledge. You thought it to be a trivial matter, while in the eyes of God it is blasphemy.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When you received it with your tongues and spoke with your mouths what you had no knowledge of, and you deemed it an easy matter while with Allah it was grievous.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When you were spreading it with your tongues and saying with your mouths things of which you had no knowledge, you considered it to be a trivial matter, but, in God's sight, it was very serious.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Behold, ye received it on your tongues, and said out of your mouths things of which ye had no knowledge; and ye thought it to be a light matter, while it was most serious in the sight of Allah.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith talaqqawnahu bialsinatikum wataqooloona biafwahikum ma laysa lakum bihi AAilmun watahsaboonahu hayyinan wahuwa AAinda Allahi AAatheemun

16.arabic: وَلَوْلَآ إِذْ سَمِعْتُمُوهُ قُلْتُم مَّا يَكُونُ لَنَآ أَن نَّتَكَلَّمَ بِهَـٰذَا سُبْحَـٰنَكَ هَـٰذَا بُهْتَـٰنٌ عَظِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Why did you not say when you heard it: "It is not for us to speak of it? God preserve us, it is a great calumny!"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And why was it not that, when you heard it, you would have said, “It does not befit us to speak regarding this; Purity is to You, O Allah – this is a great accusation.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And why, when you heard it, did you not say, 'It is not for us to speak about this; glory be to Thee! This is a mighty calumny'?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And wherefore, when ye heard it, did ye not say: it is not for us to speak thereof, hallowed be Thou! that is a slander mighty!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And why did you not, when you heard it, say? "It is not right for us to speak of this. Glory be to You (O Allah) this is a great lie."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When you heard it, you should have said, “It is not for us to repeat this. By Your glory, this is a serious slander.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Why did you not, as soon as you heard of it, say, "It is not proper for us to utter such a thing? Glory be to Allah! This is a great slander."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And why did you not, when you heard it, say: "It is not right for us to speak of this. Glory be to You (O Allah)! This is a great lie."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Wherefor, when ye heard it, said ye not: It is not for us to speak of this. Glory be to Thee (O Allah)! This is awful calumny.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And why did you not, when you heard it, say, ‘It is not for us to say such a thing. [O Allah!] You are immaculate! This is a monstrous calumny!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When you heard it, why did you not say: 'It is not right for us to speak of this. Exaltations to You! This is a mighty slander'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And why, when you heard it, did you not say, "It is not for us to speak of this. Exalted are You, [O Allah]; this is a great slander"?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Would that on hearing this report, you had said, "We have nothing to say about it. God forbid! It is a serious accusation."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And why did you not, when you heard it, say: It does not beseem us that we should talk of it; glory be to Thee! this is a great calumny?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When you heard it, why did you not say, "It is not right for us to speak of this. God forbid! This is a monstrous slander."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And why did ye not, when ye heard it, say? - "It is not right of us to speak of this: Glory to Allah! this is a most serious slander!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walawla ith samiAAtumoohu qultum ma yakoonu lana an natakallama bihatha subhanaka hatha buhtanun AAatheemun

17.arabic: يَعِظُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ أَن تَعُودُوا۟ لِمِثْلِهِۦٓ أَبَدًا إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: God counsels you not to do a thing like this, if you are believers.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Allah advises you never to speak like this again, if you have faith.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God admonishes you, that you shall never repeat the like of it again; if you are believers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Allah exhorteth you lest ye may ever revert to the like thereof, if ye are believers indeed.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Allah forbids you from it and warns you not to repeat the like of it forever, if you are believers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: God cautions you never to return to the like of it, if you are believers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Allah admonishes you that in future you should never repeat a thing like this, if you are true Believers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Allah forbids you from it and warns you not to repeat the like of it forever, if you are believers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Allah admonisheth you that ye repeat not the like thereof ever, if ye are (in truth) believers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Allah advises you lest you should ever repeat the like of it, should you be faithful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Allah exhorts you never again to repeat the like, if you are believers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Allah warns you against returning to the likes of this [conduct], ever, if you should be believers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God advises you never to do such things again if you have any faith.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Allah admonishes you that you should not return to the like of it ever again if you are believers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God warns you never to repeat the like of it again, if you are true believers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Allah doth admonish you, that ye may never repeat such (conduct), if ye are (true) Believers.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: YaAAithukumu Allahu an taAAoodoo limithlihi abadan in kuntum mumineena

18.arabic: وَيُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: God explains His commands to you clearly, for God is all-knowing and all-wise.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And Allah clearly explains the verses for you; and Allah is All Knowing, Wise.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God makes clear to you the signs; and God is All-knowing, All-wise.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And Allah expoundeth unto you the revelations and Allah is Knowing, wise.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And Allah makes the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) plain to you, and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: God explains the Verses to you. God is Knowing and Wise.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Allah makes His Revelations clear to you, and He is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And Allah makes the Ayat plain to you, and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And He expoundeth unto you the revelations. Allah is Knower, Wise.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Allah clarifies the signs for you, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Allah makes plain to you His verses, and Allah is the Knower, the Wise.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And Allah makes clear to you the verses, and Allah is Knowing and Wise.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God explains to you His revelations. He is All-knowing and All-wise.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Allah makes clear to you the communications; and Allah is Knowing, Wise.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God explains the commandments to you. God is all knowing and wise.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And Allah makes the Signs plain to you: for Allah is full of knowledge and wisdom.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wayubayyinu Allahu lakumu alayati waAllahu AAaleemun hakeemun

19.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُحِبُّونَ أَن تَشِيعَ ٱلْفَـٰحِشَةُ فِى ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: There is painful punishment in this world and the next for those who like that immorality should spread among the believers, for God knows and you do not know.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed those who wish that slander should spread among the Muslims – for them is a painful punishment in this world and in the Hereafter; and Allah knows, and you do not know.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those who love that indecency should be spread abroad concerning them that believe -- there awaits them a painful chastisement in the present world and the world to come; and God knows, and you know not.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who love that in decency should be propagated regarding those who believe, for them shall be a torment afflictive in the world and the Hereafter. And Allah knoweth, and ye know not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who like that (the crime of) illegal sexual intercourse should be propagated among those who believe, they will have a painful torment in this world and in the Hereafter. And Allah knows and you know not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who love to see immorality spread among the believers—for them is a painful punishment, in this life and in the Hereafter. God knows, and you do not know.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for those, who like that indecency should spread among the Believers, they deserve a painful punishment in this world and in the Hereafter, for Allah knows and you do not know (its consequences).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who like that Fahishah should be circulated among those who believe, they will have a painful torment in this world and in the Hereafter. And Allah knows and you know not.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! those who love that slander should be spread concerning those who believe, theirs will be a painful punishment in the world and the Hereafter. Allah knoweth. Ye know not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed those who want indecency to spread among the faithful—there is a painful punishment for them in the world and the Hereafter, and Allah knows and you do not know.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who love that indecency should be broadcast about those who believe theirs is a painful punishment in this world and in the Everlasting Life. Allah knows, and you do not know.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who like that immorality should be spread [or publicized] among those who have believed will have a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter. And Allah knows and you do not know.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who like to publicize indecency among the believers will face painful torment in this world and in the life to come. God knows what you do not know.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely (as for) those who love that scandal should circulate respecting those who believe, they shall have a grievous chastisement in this world and the hereafter; and Allah knows, while you do not know.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who desire that indecencies should spread among the believers, will have a painful chastisement in this world and the Hereafter. God knows, and you do not know.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who love (to see) scandal published broadcast among the Believers, will have a grievous Penalty in this life and in the Hereafter: Allah knows, and ye know not.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena yuhibboona an tasheeAAa alfahishatu fee allatheena amanoo lahum AAathabun aleemun fee alddunya waalakhirati waAllahu yaAAlamu waantum la taAAlamoona

20.arabic: وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But for the grace of God and His mercy (much harm would have been done); yet God is compassionate and kind.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And were it not for Allah’s munificence and His mercy upon you, and that Allah is Forgiving, Most Merciful – you would have tasted its outcome.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But for God's bounty to you and His mercy and that God is All-gentle, All-compassionate --

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had there not been Allah's grace upon you and His mercy, and that Allah was Tender and Merciful, ye had Perished.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And had it not been for the Grace of Allah and His Mercy on you, (Allah would have hastened the punishment upon you). And that Allah is full of kindness, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Were it not for God’s grace upon you, and His mercy, and that God is Clement and Merciful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: If Allah had not shown His grace and mercy to you, (this scandal would have produced very evil results): Allah is indeed very Kind and Merciful.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And had it not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy on you, and that Allah is full of kindness, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Had it not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy unto you, and that Allah is Clement, Merciful, (ye had been undone).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you, and that Allah is all-kind, all-merciful…

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If it was not for the bounty of Allah to you and His Mercy, and Allah is the Gentle, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if it had not been for the favor of Allah upon you and His mercy... and because Allah is Kind and Merciful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Were it not for the favor and mercy of God upon you (you would have faced painful punishment in this world and the life to come). God is Compassionate and Merciful.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And were it not for Allah's grace on you and His mercy, and that Allah is Compassionate, Merciful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But for the grace of God and His mercy upon you, and were not God compassionate and merciful, [you would have come to grief].

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Were it not for the grace and mercy of Allah on you, and that Allah is full of kindness and mercy, (ye would be ruined indeed).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walawla fadlu Allahi AAalaykum warahmatuhu waanna Allaha raoofun raheemun

21.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَتَّبِعُوا۟ خُطُوَٰتِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّبِعْ خُطُوَٰتِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَأْمُرُ بِٱلْفَحْشَآءِ وَٱلْمُنكَرِ ۚ وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ مَا زَكَىٰ مِنكُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ أَبَدًا وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُزَكِّى مَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O you who believe, do not follow in the footsteps of Satan, for he who follows in the footsteps of Satan will be induced by him to what is shameful and forbidden. But for the grace of God and His mercy upon you none of you would have escaped undefiled; but God makes whosoever He will grow in goodness, for God is all-hearing and all-knowing.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O People who Believe! Do not follow the footsteps of the devil; and whoever follows the footsteps of the devil – so he will only bid the indecent and the evil; and were it not for Allah’s munificence and His mercy upon you, none of you would ever become pure – but Allah purifies whomever He wills; and Allah is All Hearing, All Knowing.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O believers, follow not the steps of Satan; for whosoever follows the steps of Satan, assuredly he bids to indecency and dishonour. But for God's bounty to you and His mercy not one of you would have been pure ever; but God purifies whom He will; and God is All-hearing, All-knowing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Ye who believe! follow not the footsteps of the Satan. And whosoever followeth the footsteps of the Satan, then he only urgeth to indecency and abomination. And had there not been the grace of Allah upon you and His mercy, not one of you would ever have been cleansed; but Allah cleanseth whomsoever He Will and Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O you who believe! Follow not the footsteps of Shaitan (Satan). And whosoever follows the footsteps of Shaitan (Satan), then, verily he commands Al-Fahsha' [i.e. to commit indecency (illegal sexual intercourse, etc.)], and Al-Munkar [disbelief and polytheism (i.e. to do evil and wicked deeds; to speak or to do what is forbidden in Islam, etc.)]. And had it not been for the Grace of Allah and His Mercy on you, not one of you would ever have been pure from sins. But Allah purifies (guides to Islam) whom He wills, and Allah is All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O you who believe! Do not follow Satan’s footsteps. Whoever follows Satan’s footsteps—he advocates obscenity and immorality. Were it not for God’s grace towards you, and His mercy, not one of you would have been pure, ever. But God purifies whomever He wills. God is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: O Believers, do not follow in Satan's footsteps, for he will incite to indecency and wickedness any who will follow him. If Allah had not shown His Brace and mercy to you, none of you would have been able to cleanse yourself, for it is Allah alone Who cleanses whom He wills, and Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O you who believe! Follow not the Khutuwat of Shaytan. And whosoever follows the footsteps of Shaytan, then, verily, he commands Al-Fahsha' and the evil deeds. And had it not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy on you, not one of you would ever have been pure from sins. But Allah purifies whom He wills, and Allah is All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O ye who believe! Follow not the footsteps of the devil. Unto whomsoever followeth the footsteps of the devil, lo! he commandeth filthiness and wrong. Had it not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy unto you, not one of you would ever have grown pure. But Allah causeth whom He will to grow. And Allah is Hearer, Knower.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O you who have faith! Do not follow in Satan’s steps. Whoever follows in Satan’s steps [should know that] he indeed prompts [you to commit] indecent and wrongful acts. Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy upon you, not one of you would ever become pure. But Allah purifies whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Believers, do not follow in the steps of satan, for those who follow the steps of satan, he bids to indecency and dishonor. But for the bounty of Allah to you, and His Mercy no one of you would ever have been purified; but Allah purifies whom He will; Allah is the Hearer, the Knower.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O you who have believed, do not follow the footsteps of Satan. And whoever follows the footsteps of Satan - indeed, he enjoins immorality and wrongdoing. And if not for the favor of Allah upon you and His mercy, not one of you would have been pure, ever, but Allah purifies whom He wills, and Allah is Hearing and Knowing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Believers, do not follow the footsteps of satan; whoever does so will be made by satan to commit indecency and sin. Were it not for the favor and mercy of God, none of you would ever have been purified. God purifies whomever He wants. God is All-hearing and All-knowing.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O you who believe! do not follow the footsteps of the Shaitan, and whoever follows the footsteps of the Shaitan, then surely he bids the doing of indecency and evil; and were it not for Allah's grace upon you and His mercy, not one of you would have ever been pure, but Allah purifies whom He pleases; and Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: O you who believe, do not follow in the footsteps of Satan, and whoever follows in the footsteps of Satan should know that he enjoins only indecency and evil. But for the grace of God and His mercy upon you, not one of you would ever be purified; but God purifies whom He pleases. God is all hearing and all knowing.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye who believe! follow not Satan's footsteps: if any will follow the footsteps of Satan, he will (but) command what is shameful and wrong: and were it not for the grace and mercy of Allah on you, not one of you would ever have been pure: but Allah doth purify whom He pleases: and Allah is One Who hears and knows (all things).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo la tattabiAAoo khutuwati alshshaytani waman yattabiAA khutuwati alshshaytani fainnahu yamuru bialfahshai waalmunkari walawla fadlu Allahi AAalaykum warahmatuhu ma zaka minkum min ahadin abadan walakinna Allaha yuzakkee man yashao waAllahu sameeAAun AAaleemun

22.arabic: وَلَا يَأْتَلِ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْفَضْلِ مِنكُمْ وَٱلسَّعَةِ أَن يُؤْتُوٓا۟ أُو۟لِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينَ وَٱلْمُهَـٰجِرِينَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلْيَعْفُوا۟ وَلْيَصْفَحُوٓا۟ ۗ أَلَا تُحِبُّونَ أَن يَغْفِرَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Let not those who are men of plenty and means among you swear that they will not give to their relatives and the poor and those who leave their homes in the service of God. They should forgive and overlook (their failings). Would you not like God to forgive you? And God is forgiving and kind.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And may not those who possess superiority (in wealth) among you and possess capacity, swear not to give to the relatives and to the needy, and to immigrants in Allah's cause; and they should forgive and forbear; do you not like that Allah may forgive you? And Allah is Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Let not those of you who possess bounty and plenty swear off giving kinsmen and the poor and those who emigrate in the way of God; but let them pardon and forgive. Do you not wish that God should forgive you? God is All-forgiving, All-compassionate.
