translations.text: In houses which Allah has allowed to be raised up, and His Name to be remembered therein. In the morning and evening

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Such niches are] in mosques which Allah has ordered to be raised and that His name be mentioned therein; exalting Him within them in the morning and the evenings

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (This niche) is in the houses that God has declared to be highly respected and His Name be mentioned therein in glory in the morning and evening

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: In houses which Allah has permitted to be exalted and that His name may be remembered in them; there glorify Him therein in the mornings and the evenings,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: [They worship] in the houses which God has allowed to be raised for the remembrance of His name, morning and evening,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Lit is such a Light) in houses, which Allah hath permitted to be raised to honour; for the celebration, in them, of His name: In them is He glorified in the mornings and in the evenings, (again and again),-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fee buyootin athina Allahu an turfaAAa wayuthkara feeha ismuhu yusabbihu lahu feeha bialghuduwwi waalasali

37.arabic: رِجَالٌ لَّا تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجَـٰرَةٌ وَلَا بَيْعٌ عَن ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ وَإِقَامِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَإِيتَآءِ ٱلزَّكَوٰةِ ۙ يَخَافُونَ يَوْمًا تَتَقَلَّبُ فِيهِ ٱلْقُلُوبُ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: By men not distracted from the remembrance of God either by trade and commerce or buying and selling, who stand by their devotional obligations and pay the zakat, who fear the day when hearts and eyes would flutter with trepidation

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: (Are) Those men, whom neither any bargain nor any trade distracts from the Remembrance of Allah and from establishing the prayer and from paying the charity – they fear the day when the hearts and the eyes will be overturned.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: are men whom neither commerce nor trafficking diverts from the remembrance of God and to perform the prayer, and to pay the alms, fearing a day when hearts and eyes shall be turned about,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Men whom neither trafficking nor bargaining diverteth from the remembrance of Allah and the establishment of the prayer and the giving of the poor-rate, fearing a Day whereon upset will be the hearts and sights.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Men whom neither trade nor sale diverts them from the Remembrance of Allah (with heart and tongue), nor from performing As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), nor from giving the Zakat. They fear a Day when hearts and eyes will be overturned (from the horror of the torment of the Day of Resurrection).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: By men who neither trading nor commerce distracts them from God’s remembrance, and from performing the prayers, and from giving alms. They fear a Day when hearts and sights are overturned.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: as are not diverted by trade and merchandise from remembering Him and from establishing Salat and paying Zakat, for they fear the Day when the hearts will be overturned and the eyes will become petrified.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Men whom neither trade nor business diverts from the remembrance of Allah nor from performing the Salah nor from giving the Zakah. They fear a Day when hearts and eyes will be overturned.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Men whom neither merchandise nor sale beguileth from remembrance of Allah and constancy in prayer and paying to the poor their due; who fear a day when hearts and eyeballs will be overturned;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: by men whom neither trade nor bargaining distracts from the remembrance of Allah and the maintenance of prayer and the giving of zakat. They are fearful of a day wherein the hearts and the sights will be transformed,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: are men who exalt Him there, whom neither trade nor sale can divert from the remembrance of Allah, and establish the prayers, and pay the obligatorycharity; fearing a Day when hearts and eyes shall be turned about,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Are] men whom neither commerce nor sale distracts from the remembrance of Allah and performance of prayer and giving of zakah. They fear a Day in which the hearts and eyes will [fearfully] turn about -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: by people, who can neither be diverted by merchandise nor bargaining from worshipping God, saying their prayers and paying religious tax. They do these things, for they are afraid of the day when all hearts and eyes will undergo terrible unrest and crisis.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Men whom neither merchandise nor selling diverts from the remembrance of Allah and the keeping up of prayer and the giving of poor-rate; they fear a day in which the hearts and eyes shall turn about;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: people who are not distracted by trade or commerce from the remembrance of God and the observance of prayer and the payment of the zakat -- fearing a Day when hearts and eyes will be convulsed,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: By men whom neither traffic nor merchandise can divert from the Remembrance of Allah, nor from regular Prayer, nor from the practice of regular Charity: Their (only) fear is for the Day when hearts and eyes will be transformed (in a world wholly new),-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Rijalun la tulheehim tijaratun wala bayAAun AAan thikri Allahi waiqami alssalati waeetai alzzakati yakhafoona yawman tataqallabu feehi alquloobu waalabsaru

38.arabic: لِيَجْزِيَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ أَحْسَنَ مَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَيَزِيدَهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَآءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: That God may reward them for the best of their deeds, and bestow more on them of His bounty, for God gives whom He please without measure.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In order that Allah may reward them for their best deeds and by His munificence, bestow upon them an increased reward; and Allah gives sustenance to whomever He wills, without account.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: that God may recompense them for their fairest works and give them increase of His bounty; and God provides whomsoever He will, without reckoning.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: That Allah may recompense them the best for that which they worked and may increase unto them of His grace, and Allah provideth for whomsoever He will without measure.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: That Allah may reward them according to the best of their deeds, and add even more for them out of His Grace. And Allah provides without measure to whom He wills.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: God will reward them according to the best of what they did, and He will increase them from His bounty. God provides for whomever He wills without reckoning.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (And they behave like this) so that Allah may reward them for their excellent deeds and, in addition to it, show His favour to them out of His bounty: Allah provides without stint for anyone He pleases.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: That Allah may reward them according to the best of their deeds, and add even more for them out of His grace. And Allah provides without measure to whom He wills.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That Allah may reward them with the best of what they did, and increase reward for them of His bounty. Allah giveth blessings without stint to whom He will.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: so that Allah may reward them by the best of what they have done, and enhance them out of His grace, and Allah provides for whomever He wishes without any reckoning.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: that Allah will recompense them for the finest deeds they did and increase them from His bounty. Allah provides without measure to whom He will.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That Allah may reward them [according to] the best of what they did and increase them from His bounty. And Allah gives provision to whom He wills without account.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (They worship Him) so that God will reward their best deeds and give them more through His favors. God gives sustenance to whomever He wants without account.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: That Allah may give them the best reward of what they have done, and give them more out of His grace; and Allah gives sustenance to whom He pleases without measure.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: so that God may reward them according to the best of their deeds and give them more out of His bounty. God provides for whoever He wills without measure.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: That Allah may reward them according to the best of their deeds, and add even more for them out of His Grace: for Allah doth provide for those whom He will, without measure.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Liyajziyahumu Allahu ahsana ma AAamiloo wayazeedahum min fadlihi waAllahu yarzuqu man yashao bighayri hisabin

39.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ كَسَرَابٍۭ بِقِيعَةٍ يَحْسَبُهُ ٱلظَّمْـَٔانُ مَآءً حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَهُۥ لَمْ يَجِدْهُ شَيْـًٔا وَوَجَدَ ٱللَّهَ عِندَهُۥ فَوَفَّىٰهُ حِسَابَهُۥ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As for those who disbelieve, their deeds are like a mirage in the desert which the thirsty takes for water till he reaches it to find that there was nothing, and finds God with him who settles his account, for God is swift at the reckoning.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And those who disbelieved – their deeds are like a mirage in the wasteland, so the thirsty may believe it to be water; to the extent that when he came close to it, he found it is nothing and found Allah close to him, so He filled his account; and Allah is Swift At Taking Account.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And as for the unbelievers, their works are as a mirage in a spacious plain which the man athirst supposes to be water, till, when he comes to it, he finds it is nothing; there indeed he finds God, and He pays him his account in full; (and God is swift at the reckoning.)

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And those who disbelieve--their works are like a mirage in a desert which the thirsty deemeth to be water, until when he cometh thereto he findeth not aught, and findeth Allahs with himself, and He payeth him his account in full; and Allah is swift at reckoning.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: As for those who disbelieve, their deeds are like a mirage in a desert. The thirsty one thinks it to be water, until he comes up to it, he finds it to be nothing, but he finds Allah with him, Who will pay him his due (Hell). And Allah is Swift in taking account.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As for those who disbelieve, their works are like a mirage in a desert. The thirsty assumes it is to be water. Until, when he has reached it, he finds it to be nothing, but there he finds God, Who settles his account in full. God is swift in reckoning.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (On the other hand,) the deeds of those who disbelieved, maybe likened to a mirage in a waterless desert, which the thirsty one took for water; but when he reached there he found nothing to drink; nay, he found there Allah Who settled his full account, and Allah is very swift at reckoning.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: As for those who disbelieved, their deeds are like a mirage in a Qi`ah. The thirsty one thinks it to be water until he comes up to it, he finds it to be nothing; but he finds Allah with him, Who will pay him his due. And Allah is swift in taking account.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: As for those who disbelieve, their deeds are as a mirage in a desert. The thirsty one supposeth it to be water till he cometh unto it and findeth it naught, and findeth, in the place thereof, Allah Who payeth him his due; and Allah is swift at reckoning.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for the faithless, their works are like a mirage in a plain, which the thirsty man supposes to be water. When he comes to it, he finds it to be nothing; but there he finds Allah, who will pay him his full account, and Allah is swift at reckoning.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: As for the unbelievers, their works are like a mirage in the wilderness. The thirsty person thinks it is water, but when he comes near he finds that it is nothing. He finds Allah there, who pays him his account in full. Allah is Swift in reckoning.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But those who disbelieved - their deeds are like a mirage in a lowland which a thirsty one thinks is water until, when he comes to it, he finds it is nothing but finds Allah before Him, and He will pay him in full his due; and Allah is swift in account.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The deeds of the unbelievers are like a mirage which a thirsty man thinks is water until he goes near and finds nothing. Instead he finds God who gives him his due recompense. God's reckoning is swift.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And (as for) those who disbelieve, their deeds are like the mirage in a desert, which the thirsty man deems to be water; until when he comes to it he finds it to be naught, and there he finds Allah, so He pays back to him his reckoning in full; and Allah is quick in reckoning;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: As for those who deny the truth, their works are like a mirage in a desert. The thirsty traveller thinks it to be water, but when he comes near, he finds it to be nothing. He finds God there, who pays him his account in full. God is swift in His reckoning.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But the Unbelievers,- their deeds are like a mirage in sandy deserts, which the man parched with thirst mistakes for water; until when he comes up to it, he finds it to be nothing: But he finds Allah (ever) with him, and Allah will pay him his account: and Allah is swift in taking account.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waallatheena kafaroo aAAmaluhum kasarabin biqeeAAatin yahsabuhu alththamanu maan hatta itha jaahu lam yajidhu shayan wawajada Allaha AAindahu fawaffahu hisabahu waAllahu sareeAAu alhisabi

40.arabic: أَوْ كَظُلُمَـٰتٍ فِى بَحْرٍ لُّجِّىٍّ يَغْشَىٰهُ مَوْجٌ مِّن فَوْقِهِۦ مَوْجٌ مِّن فَوْقِهِۦ سَحَابٌ ۚ ظُلُمَـٰتٌۢ بَعْضُهَا فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ إِذَآ أَخْرَجَ يَدَهُۥ لَمْ يَكَدْ يَرَىٰهَا ۗ وَمَن لَّمْ يَجْعَلِ ٱللَّهُ لَهُۥ نُورًا فَمَا لَهُۥ مِن نُّورٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or like darkness in a wide, wide sea, waves surging upon waves, with clouds overhanging, darkness on darkness. If you stretch your hand, you could hardly see it. For him whom God does not give any light, there is no light.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or like realms of darkness on a deep sea, which is covered by a wave, the wave covered by another wave, and above it is a cloud; layers of darkness upon darkness; if he removes his hand it does not seem visible; and the one to whom Allah does not provide light – there is no light for him anywhere.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: or they are as shadows upon a sea obscure covered by a billow above which is a billow above which are clouds, shadows piled one upon another; when he puts forth his hand, wellnigh he cannot see it. And to whomsoever God assigns no light, no light has he.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Or, like the darknesses in a sea deep; there covereth it a wave from above it, a wave from above it, above which is a cloud: darknesses one above anot her: when he putteth out his hand well-nigh he seeth it not. And upto whomsoever Allah shall not appoint a light, his shall be no light.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or [the state of a disbeliever] is like the darkness in a vast deep sea, overwhelmed with a great wave topped by a great wave, topped by dark clouds, darkness, one above another, if a man stretches out his hand, he can hardly see it! And he for whom Allah has not appointed light, for him there is no light.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or like utter darkness in a vast ocean, covered by waves, above which are waves, above which is fog. Darkness upon darkness. If he brings out his hand, he will hardly see it. He to whom God has not granted a light has no light.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or (their efforts may be likened to those of a man trying to swim in) a deep dark ocean, covered with billows, one over the other, and above it a cloud: darkness upon darkness: so much so that if he stretches out his hand, he cannot see it. There is no light for the one whom Allah does not give light.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or like the darkness in a vast deep sea, overwhelmed with waves topped by waves, topped by dark clouds, (layers of) darkness upon darkness: if a man stretches out his hand, he can hardly see it! And he for whom Allah has not appointed light, for him there is no light.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or as darkness on a vast, abysmal sea. There covereth him a wave, above which is a wave, above which is a cloud. Layer upon layer of darkness. When he holdeth out his hand he scarce can see it. And he for whom Allah hath not appointed light, for him there is no light.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Or like the manifold darkness in a deep sea, covered by billow upon billow, overcast by clouds; manifold [layers of] darkness, one on top of another: when he brings out his hand, he can hardly see it. One whom Allah has not granted any light has no light.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, they are like darkness upon a deep sea covered with a wave above which is another wave, above which are clouds, darkness piled one upon the other; when he stretches out his hand he can scarcely see it. Indeed, to whomsoever Allah assigns no light, he shall have no light.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or [they are] like darknesses within an unfathomable sea which is covered by waves, upon which are waves, over which are clouds - darknesses, some of them upon others. When one puts out his hand [therein], he can hardly see it. And he to whom Allah has not granted light - for him there is no light.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Or it (the deeds of the unbelievers) are like the darkness of a deep, stormy sea with layers of giant waves, covered by dark clouds. It is darkness upon darkness whereby even if one stretches out his hands he can not see them. One can have no light unless God gives him light.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or like utter darkness in the deep sea: there covers it a wave above which is another wave, above which is a cloud, (layers of) utter darkness one above another; when he holds out his hand, he is almost unable to see it; and to whomsoever Allah does not give light, he has no light.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Or like darkness on a deep ocean covered by waves billowing over waves and overcast with clouds: darkness upon darkness. If he stretches out his hand, he can scarcely see it. Indeed, the man from whom God withholds His light shall find no light at all.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or (the Unbelievers' state) is like the depths of darkness in a vast deep ocean, overwhelmed with billow topped by billow, topped by (dark) clouds: depths of darkness, one above another: if a man stretches out his hands, he can hardly see it! for any to whom Allah giveth not light, there is no light!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Aw kathulumatin fee bahrin lujjiyyin yaghshahu mawjun min fawqihi mawjun min fawqihi sahabun thulumatun baAAduha fawqa baAAdin itha akhraja yadahu lam yakad yaraha waman lam yajAAali Allahu lahu nooran fama lahu min noorin

41.arabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُۥ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلطَّيْرُ صَـٰٓفَّـٰتٍ ۖ كُلٌّ قَدْ عَلِمَ صَلَاتَهُۥ وَتَسْبِيحَهُۥ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Have you not seen that all those who are in the heavens and the earth, and the birds on the wing, sing the praises of God. Each one knows its obligations and its duties, and God knows whatever they do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Have you not seen that all those who are in the heavens and the earth praise Allah, and the birds with their wings spread (also praise Him)? Each one has learnt its prayers and its words of praise; and Allah knows their deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Hast thou not seen how that whatsoever is in the heavens and in the earth extols God, and the birds spreading their wings? Each -- He knows its prayer and its extolling; and God knows the things they do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Beholdest thou not that Allah --those hallow Him whosoever is in the heavens and the earth and the birds with wings outspread? Surely each one knoweth his prayer and his hallowing; and Allah is the Knower of that which they do.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: See you not (O Muhammad SAW) that Allah, He it is Whom glorify whosoever is in the heavens and the earth, and the birds with wings out-spread (in their flight). Of each one He (Allah) knows indeed his Salat (prayer) and his glorification, [or everyone knows his Salat (prayer) and his glorification], and Allah is All-Aware of what they do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Do you not realize that God is glorified by whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and even by the birds in formation? Each knows its prayer and its manner of praise. God knows well what they do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do you not observe that all those who are in the heavens and the earth, and the birds with outspread wings, glorify Allah? Each one knows the mode of its prayer and glorification, and Allah has full knowledge of all they do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: See you not that Allah, He it is Whom glorify whosoever is in the heavens and the earth, and the birds with wings outspread Of each one He knows indeed his Salah and his glorification; and Allah is All-Aware of what they do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Hast thou not seen that Allah, He it is Whom all who are in the heavens and the earth praise, and the birds in their flight? Of each He knoweth verily the worship and the praise; and Allah is Aware of what they do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Have you not regarded that Allah is glorified by everyone in the heavens and the earth, and the birds spreading their wings. Each knows his prayer and glorification, and Allah knows best what they do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Have you not seen how Allah is exalted by those in the heavens and earth, and the birds with outspread wings? He knows its prayers and its exaltations and Allah has knowledge of the things they do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Do you not see that Allah is exalted by whomever is within the heavens and the earth and [by] the birds with wings spread [in flight]? Each [of them] has known his [means of] prayer and exalting [Him], and Allah is Knowing of what they do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Have you not considered that all that is between the heavens and the earth glorifies God and that the birds spread their wings in the air to glorify God? He knows everyone's prayers and praising; God has absolute knowledge of what they do.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Do you not see that Allah is He Whom do glorify all those who are in the heavens and the earth, and the (very) birds with expanded wings? He knows the prayer of each one and its glorification, and Allah is Cognizant of what they do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: [Prophet], do you not see that all those who are in the heavens and on earth praise God, as do the birds with wings outstretched? Each knows his own mode of prayer and glorification: God has full knowledge of all that they do.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Seest thou not that it is Allah Whose praises all beings in the heavens and on earth do celebrate, and the birds (of the air) with wings outspread? Each one knows its own (mode of) prayer and praise. And Allah knows well all that they do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alam tara anna Allaha yusabbihu lahu man fee alssamawati waalardi waalttayru saffatin kullun qad AAalima salatahu watasbeehahu waAllahu AAaleemun bima yafAAaloona

42.arabic: وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: For God's is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and the returning is to God.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And for Allah only is the kingship of the heavens and the earth; and towards Allah is the return.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: To God belongs the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and to Him is the homecoming.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And Allah's is the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and unto Allah is the return.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and to Allah is the return (of all).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: To God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and to God is the ultimate return.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The kingdom of the heavens and the earth belongs to Allah alone, and all shall have to return to Him.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and to Allah is the return.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And unto Allah belongeth the Sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and unto Allah is the journeying.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and toward Allah is the destination.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: To Allah belongs the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth. To Him is the arrival.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and to Allah is the destination.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: To God belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and to Him do all things return.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Allah's is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and to Allah is the eventual coming.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: To God belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and to God shall all things return.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Yea, to Allah belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; and to Allah is the final goal (of all).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walillahi mulku alssamawati waalardi waila Allahi almaseeru

43.arabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُزْجِى سَحَابًا ثُمَّ يُؤَلِّفُ بَيْنَهُۥ ثُمَّ يَجْعَلُهُۥ رُكَامًا فَتَرَى ٱلْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلَـٰلِهِۦ وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مِن جِبَالٍ فِيهَا مِنۢ بَرَدٍ فَيُصِيبُ بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَصْرِفُهُۥ عَن مَّن يَشَآءُ ۖ يَكَادُ سَنَا بَرْقِهِۦ يَذْهَبُ بِٱلْأَبْصَـٰرِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Have you not seen that God drives the clouds, then joins them together and puts them fold on fold. Then you see the rain fall through them; and He sends down hail from the sky where there are mountains of it, and strikes those with it whom He will, and wards it off from whomsoever He please. His lightning could snatch away their eyes.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Have you not seen that Allah slowly transports the clouds, then gathers them together, then heaps them together, so you see the rain coming forth from between them? And from the sky He sends down hail from the mountains of ice, then sends them upon whomever He wills, and averts them from whomever He wills; it is close that the flash of its lightning take away the eyesight.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'hast thou not seen how God drives the clouds, then composes them, then converts them into a mass, then thou seest the rain issuing out of the midst of them? And He sends down out of heaven mountains, wherein is hail, so that He smites whom He will with it, and turns it aside from whom He will; wellnigh the gleam of His lightning snatches away the sight.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Beholdest thou not that Allah driveth a cloud along, then combineth it, then maketh it a heap, and then thou beholdest the fine rain come forth from the interstices thereof? And He sendeth down from the heaven mountains wherein is hail, then afflicteth therewith whomsoever He will, and averteth it from whomsoever He will: wellnigh the flash of His lightening taketh away the sights.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: See you not that Allah drives the clouds gently, then joins them together, then makes them into a heap of layers, and you see the rain comes forth from between them. And He sends down from the sky hail (like) mountains, (or there are in the heaven mountains of hail from where He sends down hail), and strike therewith whom He will, and averts it from whom He wills. The vivid flash of its (clouds) lightning nearly blinds the sight. [Tafsir At-Tabari].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Have you not seen how God propels the clouds, then brings them together, then piles them into a heap, and you see rain drops emerging from its midst? How He brings down loads of hail from the sky, striking with it whomever He wills, and diverting it from whomever He wills? The flash of its lightening almost snatches the sight away.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do you not observe that Allah makes the cloud move gently then joins its pieces together: then gathers it into a mass of thick cloud: then you see that rain-drops fall down from its midst: and He sends down hail out of the high up mountains in the heaven: then He smites with it whom He wills and turns it away from whom He pleases: then a flash of lightning from it dazzles the eyes.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: See you not that Allah drives the clouds gently, then joins them together, then makes them into a heap of layers, and you see the rain come forth from between them; and He sends down from the sky, from mountains in it of ice, and strikes therewith whom He wills, and averts it from whom He wills. The vivid flash of its (clouds) lightning nearly blinds the sight.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Hast thou not seen how Allah wafteth the clouds, then gathereth them, then maketh them layers, and thou seest the rain come forth from between them; He sendeth down from the heaven mountains wherein is hail, and smiteth therewith whom He will, and averteth it from whom He will. The flashing of His lightning all but snatcheth away the sight.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Have you not regarded that Allah drives the clouds, then He composes them, then He piles them up, whereat you see the rain issuing from its midst? And He sends down hail from the sky, out of the mountains that are in it, and He strikes with it whomever He wishes, and turns it away from whomever He wishes. The brilliance of its lightening almost takes away the sight.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Have you not seen how Allah drives the clouds, then gathers them and converts them into a mass, then you see rain coming from the midst of them? And He sends down out of heaven mountains in which there is hail, pelting with it whom He will, and turning it away from whom He will. The flash of its lightning almost snatches away the sight.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Do you not see that Allah drives clouds? Then He brings them together, then He makes them into a mass, and you see the rain emerge from within it. And He sends down from the sky, mountains [of clouds] within which is hail, and He strikes with it whom He wills and averts it from whom He wills. The flash of its lightening almost takes away the eyesight.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do you not see that God moves the clouds gently, brings them together, piles them up, and then you can see the rain coming from them. He sends down hailstones from the mountains in the sky. With them He strikes or protects from them whomever He wants. The lightening can almost take away the sight.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Do you not see that Allah drives along the clouds, then gathers them together, then piles them up, so that you see the rain coming forth from their midst? And He sends down of the clouds that are (like) mountains wherein is hail, afflicting therewith whom He pleases and turning it away from whom He pleases; the flash of His lightning almost takes away the sight.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do you not see how God drives the clouds, then joins them together, then piles them into layers and then you see the rain pour from their midst? He sends down from the skies mountainous masses [of clouds] charged with hail, and He makes it fall on whom He will, and turns it away from whom He pleases. The flash of His lightning may well-nigh take away the sight.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Seest thou not that Allah makes the clouds move gently, then joins them together, then makes them into a heap? - then wilt thou see rain issue forth from their midst. And He sends down from the sky mountain masses (of clouds) wherein is hail: He strikes therewith whom He pleases and He turns it away from whom He pleases, the vivid flash of His lightning well-nigh blinds the sight.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alam tara anna Allaha yuzjee sahaban thumma yuallifu baynahu thumma yajAAaluhu rukaman fatara alwadqa yakhruju min khilalihi wayunazzilu mina alssamai min jibalin feeha min baradin fayuseebu bihi man yashao wayasrifuhu AAan man yashao yakadu sana barqihi yathhabu bialabsari

44.arabic: يُقَلِّبُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبْرَةً لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: It is God who alternates night and day. There is surely a lesson in this for men of sight.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Allah alternates the day and the night; indeed in this is a lesson for those who can perceive.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God turns about the day and the night; surely in that is a lesson for those who have eyes.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Allah turneth the night and the day over and over; verily therein is a lesson for men of insight.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Allah causes the night and the day to succeed each other (i.e. if the day is gone, the night comes, and if the night is gone, the day comes, and so on). Truly, in these things is indeed a lesson for those who have insight.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: God alternates the night and the day. In that is a lesson for those who have insight.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He alternates the day and the night: there is indeed a lesson in it for those who have observing eyes.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Allah causes the night and the day to succeed each other. Truly, in this is indeed a lesson for those who have insight.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Allah causeth the revolution of the day and the night. Lo! herein is indeed a lesson for those who see.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Allah alternates the night and the day. There is indeed a lesson in that for those who have insight.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Allah turns about the night and the day (to succeed one another); surely, in this there is a lesson for those who have eyes.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Allah alternates the night and the day. Indeed in that is a lesson for those who have vision.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God alternates the night and the day. In this there is a lesson for the people of understanding.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Allah turns over the night and the day; most surely there is a lesson in this for those who have sight.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God alternates the night and the day -- truly, in this there is a lesson for men of insight.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: It is Allah Who alternates the Night and the Day: verily in these things is an instructive example for those who have vision!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Yuqallibu Allahu allayla waalnnahara inna fee thalika laAAibratan liolee alabsari

45.arabic: وَٱللَّهُ خَلَقَ كُلَّ دَآبَّةٍ مِّن مَّآءٍ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مَّن يَمْشِى عَلَىٰ بَطْنِهِۦ وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَمْشِى عَلَىٰ رِجْلَيْنِ وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَمْشِى عَلَىٰٓ أَرْبَعٍ ۚ يَخْلُقُ ٱللَّهُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: God created every moving thing from water: One crawls on its belly, one walks on two legs, another moves on four. God creates whatsoever He will. Indeed God has power over everything.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And Allah created every animal on earth, from water; so among them is a kind that moves upon its belly; and among them is a kind that moves upon two legs; and among them a kind that moves upon four legs; Allah creates whatever He wills; indeed Allah is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God has created every beast of water, and some of them go upon their bellies, and some of them go upon two feet, and some of them go upon four; God creates whatever He will; God is powerful over everything.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Allah hath created every moving creature of water; of them is one that walketh upon his belly. and of them is one that walketh upon its two feet; and of them is one that walketh upon four. Allah createth whatsoever He listeth; verily Allah is over everything Potent.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Allah has created every moving (living) creature from water. Of them there are some that creep on their bellies, some that walk on two legs, and some that walk on four. Allah creates what He wills. Verily! Allah is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: God created every living creature from water. Some of them crawl on their bellies, and some walk on two feet, and others walk on four. God creates whatever He wills. God is Capable of everything.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And Allah created every creature from a sort of water: of them some one crawls upon its belly: another walks on two legs and still another on four; Allah creates whatever He wills for He has power over everything

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Allah has created every moving creature from water. Of them there are some that creep on their bellies, and some that walk on two legs, and some that walk on four. Allah creates what He wills. Verily, Allah is able to do all things.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Allah hath created every animal of water. Of them is (a kind) that goeth upon its belly and (a kind) that goeth upon two legs and (a kind) that goeth upon four. Allah createth what He will. Lo! Allah is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Allah created every animal from water. Among them are some that creep upon their bellies, and among them are some that walk on two feet, and among them are some that walk on four. Allah creates whatever He wishes. Indeed Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Allah created everything that walks from water. Some creep upon their bellies, others walk on two feet, and others walk on four. Allah creates whatever He will. Allah is Powerful over everything.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Allah has created every [living] creature from water. And of them are those that move on their bellies, and of them are those that walk on two legs, and of them are those that walk on four. Allah creates what He wills. Indeed, Allah is over all things competent.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God has created every living being from water: Some of them creep on their bellies; some walk on two feet and some of them walk on four legs. God creates whatever He wants. He has power over all things.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Allah has created from water every living creature: so of them is that which walks upon its belly, and of them is that which walks upon two feet, and of them is that which walks upon four; Allah creates what He pleases; surely Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God created every creature from water. Some crawl upon their bellies, others walk on two legs, and others walk on four. God creates what He pleases. He has power over all things.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And Allah has created every animal from water: of them there are some that creep on their bellies; some that walk on two legs; and some that walk on four. Allah creates what He wills for verily Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WaAllahu khalaqa kulla dabbatin min main faminhum man yamshee AAala batnihi waminhum man yamshee AAala rijlayni waminhum man yamshee AAala arbaAAin yakhluqu Allahu ma yashao inna Allaha AAala kulli shayin qadeerun

46.arabic: لَّقَدْ أَنزَلْنَآ ءَايَـٰتٍ مُّبَيِّنَـٰتٍ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We have surely sent down clear signs. It is God who guides whom He will to the path that is straight.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We indeed sent down clear verses; and Allah may guide whomever He wills to the Straight Path.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Now We have sent down signs making all clear; God guides whomsoever He will to a straight path.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Assuredly We have sent down revelations illuminating: and Allah guideth whomsoever He listeth unto a path straight.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: We have indeed sent down (in this Quran) manifest Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, lawful and unlawful things, and the set boundries of Islamic religion, etc. that make things clear showing the Right Path of Allah). And Allah guides whom He wills to a Straight Path (i.e. to Allah's religion of Islamic Monotheism).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We sent down enlightening revelations, and God guides whomever He wills to a straight path.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We have sent down Revelations that make the reality quite plain; however, Allah guides to the straight path whomsoever He pleases.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: We have indeed sent down manifest Ayat. And Allah guides whom He wills to the straight path.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Verily We have sent down revelations and explained them. Allah guideth whom He will unto a straight path.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly We have sent down illuminating signs, and Allah guides whomever He wishes to a straight path.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We have sent down clarifying verses. Allah guides whom He will to a Straight Path.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: We have certainly sent down distinct verses. And Allah guides whom He wills to a straight path.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We have revealed illustrious revelations. God guides to the right path whomever He wants.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Certainly We have revealed clear communications, and Allah guides whom He pleases to the right way.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We have sent down revelations clearly showing the truth. God guides whom He wills to the straight path.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We have indeed sent down signs that make things manifest: and Allah guides whom He wills to a way that is straight.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Laqad anzalna ayatin mubayyinatin waAllahu yahdee man yashao ila siratin mustaqeemin

47.arabic: وَيَقُولُونَ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَبِٱلرَّسُولِ وَأَطَعْنَا ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّىٰ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ ۚ وَمَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They say they believe in God and the Prophet, and have come to believe, yet a section of them turns back even after this; and these are not believers.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And they say, “We have accepted faith in Allah and His Noble Messenger, and we obey”; then after that a group among them turns away; and they are not Muslims.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They say, 'We believe in God and the Messenger, and we obey.' Then after that a party of them turn away; those -- they are not believers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they say: we have believed in Allah and in the apostle and we have obeyed; then there backslide a party of them thereafter: and these are not believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They (hypocrites) say: "We have believed in Allah and in the Messenger (Muhammad SAW), and we obey," then a party of them turn away thereafter, such are not believers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they say, “We have believed in God and the Messenger, and we obey,” but some of them turn away afterwards. These are not believers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: These people say "We have believed in Allah and the Messenger and we have submitted"; but soon after this, some of them turn away (from obedience): such people are not true Believers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They say: "We have believed in Allah and in the Messenger, and we obey," then a party of them turn away thereafter, such are not believers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And they say: We believe in Allah and the messenger, and we obey; then after that a faction of them turn away. Such are not believers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They say, ‘We have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and we obey.’ Then, after that, a part of them refuse to comply, and they do not have faith.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They say: 'We believe in Allah and the Messenger and obey' But a party of them turn away after this. Those are not believers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But the hypocrites say, "We have believed in Allah and in the Messenger, and we obey"; then a party of them turns away after that. And those are not believers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They say, "We have believed in God and the Messenger and we have obeyed them." Then a group of them turn away from their (belief). They are not believers.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they say: We believe in Allah and in the apostle and we obey; then a party of them turn back after this, and these are not believers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They say, "We believe in God and in the Messenger, and we obey." But then, even after that a group of them will turn away. Those are surely not believers

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They say, "We believe in Allah and in the messenger, and we obey": but even after that, some of them turn away: they are not (really) Believers.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wayaqooloona amanna biAllahi wabialrrasooli waataAAna thumma yatawalla fareequn minhum min baAAdi thalika wama olaika bialmumineena

48.arabic: وَإِذَا دُعُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When they are called to God and His Prophet, that he may judge between them, a section of them turns away.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when called towards Allah and His Noble Messenger in order that the Noble Messenger may judge between them, thereupon a group among them turns away.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When they are called to God and His Messenger that he may judge between them, lo, a party of them are swerving aside;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when they are called to Allah and His apostle that he may judge between them, lo! a party of them are averters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when they are called to Allah (i.e. His Words, the Quran) and His Messenger (SAW), to judge between them, lo! a party of them refuse (to come) and turn away.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when they are called to God and His Messenger, in order to judge between them, some of them refuse.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When they are called to Allah and His Messenger so that the Messenger may judge between them, a party of them turns away.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when they are called to Allah and His Messenger, to judge between them, lo! a party of them refuses and turns away.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when they appeal unto Allah and His messenger to judge between them, lo! a faction of them are averse;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When they are summoned to Allah and His Apostle that He may judge between them, behold, a part of them turn aside.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when they are called to Allah and His Messenger so that he judges between them, a party of them swerve away.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when they are called to [the words of] Allah and His Messenger to judge between them, at once a party of them turns aside [in refusal].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When they are called to God and His Messenger so that they will judge among them, suddenly, some of them turn away.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when they are called to Allah and His Apostle that he may judge between them, lo! a party of them turn aside.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and when they are called to God and His Messenger so that he may judge between them, some of them turn away.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When they are summoned to Allah and His messenger, in order that He may judge between them, behold some of them decline (to come).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waitha duAAoo ila Allahi warasoolihi liyahkuma baynahum itha fareequn minhum muAAridoona

49.arabic: وَإِن يَكُن لَّهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ يَأْتُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ مُذْعِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Had right been on their side they would have come to him submissively.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if the judgement is in their favour, they come towards it willingly.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: but if they are in the right, they will come to him submissively.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And if right had been theirs, they would have come to him readily.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But if the right is with them, they come to him willingly with submission.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But if justice is on their side, they accept it willingly.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: However, if the truth be on their side, they come towards the Messenger in all obedience.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But if the truth is on their side, they come to him willingly with submission.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But if right had been with them they would have come unto him willingly.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But if justice be on their side, they come compliantly to him.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If the right is theirs, they would have hastened to him obediently.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But if the right is theirs, they come to him in prompt obedience.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If right was on their side, they would come quickly.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if the truth be on their side, they come to him quickly, obedient.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But if the truth happens to be to their liking, they are quite willing to accept it!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But if the right is on their side, they come to him with all submission.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wain yakun lahumu alhaqqu yatoo ilayhi muthAAineena

50.arabic: أَفِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ أَمِ ٱرْتَابُوٓا۟ أَمْ يَخَافُونَ أَن يَحِيفَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَرَسُولُهُۥ ۚ بَلْ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Is there a malady in their hearts, or they are deluded, or afraid that God and His Prophet would be unjust in dealing with them? Not so; they are themselves unjust.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Are their hearts diseased, or do they have doubts, or do they fear that Allah and His Noble Messenger will oppress them? In fact they themselves are the unjust.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: What, is there sickness in their hearts, or are they in doubt, or do they fear that God may be unjust towards them and His Messenger? Nay, but those -- they are the evildoers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Is in their hearts a disease? or doubt they? or fear they that Allah shall misjudge them, as also His apostle? Aye! these are the very wrong-doers!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Is there a disease in their hearts? Or do they doubt or fear lest Allah and His Messenger (SAW) should wrong them in judgement. Nay, it is they themselves who are the Zalimun (polytheists, hypocrites and wrong-doers, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Is there sickness in their hearts? Or are they suspicious? Or do they fear that God may do them injustice? Or His Messenger? In fact, they themselves are the unjust.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Are their hearts afflicted with the disease (of hypocrisy)? Or, are they in doubt? Or, do they fear that Allah and His Messenger will be unjust to them? In fact, they themselves are unjust.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Is there a disease in their hearts Or do they doubt or fear lest Allah and His Messenger should wrong them in judgement. Nay, it is they themselves who are the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Is there in their hearts a disease, or have they doubts, or fear they lest Allah and His messenger should wrong them in judgment? Nay, but such are evil-doers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Is there a sickness in their hearts? Do they have doubts, or fear that Allah and His Apostle will be unjust to them? Indeed, it is they who are the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Is there a sickness in their hearts, or, are they in doubt? Do they fear that Allah and His Messenger will be unjust? No, but those they are the harmdoers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Is there disease in their hearts? Or have they doubted? Or do they fear that Allah will be unjust to them, or His Messenger? Rather, it is they who are the wrongdoers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Are their hearts sick? Do they have doubts or are they afraid that God and His Messenger may do injustice to them? In fact, they, themselves, are unjust.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Is there in their hearts a disease, or are they in doubt, or do they fear that Allah and His Apostle will act wrongfully towards them? Nay! they themselves are the unjust.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Is there a sickness in their hearts, or are they full of doubt? Or do they fear that God and His Messenger will be unjust to them? The truth is that they themselves are wrongdoers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Is it that there is a disease in their hearts? or do they doubt, or are they in fear, that Allah and His Messenger will deal unjustly with them? Nay, it is they themselves who do wrong.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afee quloobihim maradun ami irtaboo am yakhafoona an yaheefa Allahu AAalayhim warasooluhu bal olaika humu alththalimoona

51.arabic: إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The answer of the believers when they are called to God and His Apostle that he may judge between them, is: "We hear and obey." And they are the ones who will prosper.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The saying of the Muslims when they are called towards Allah and His Noble Messenger in order that the Noble Messenger judge between them is that they submit, “We hear and we obey”; and it is they who have attained the goal.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: All that the believers say, when they are called to God and His Messenger, that he may judge between them, is that they say, 'We hear, and we obey'; those -- they are the prosperers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: -The only saying of the believers when they were called to Allah and His apostle thathe might judge between them was that they said: We hear and we obey. And these! they are the very ones blissful.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The only saying of the faithful believers, when they are called to Allah (His Words, the Quran) and His Messenger (SAW), to judge between them, is that they say: "We hear and we obey." And such are the prosperous ones (who will live forever in Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The response of the believers, when they are called to God and His Messenger in order to judge between them, is to say, “We hear and we obey.” These are the successful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As regards the Believers, when they are called towards Allah and His Messenger so that the Messenger may judge between them, they say, "We have heard and obeyed"; such are the people who attain true success,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The only saying of the faithful believers, when they are called to Allah and His Messenger, to judge between them, is that they say: "We hear and we obey." And such are the successful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The saying of (all true) believers when they appeal unto Allah and His messenger to judge between them is only that they say: We hear and we obey. And such are the successful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: All the response of the faithful, when they are summoned to Allah and His Apostle that He may judge between them, is to say, ‘We hear and obey.’ It is they who are the felicitous.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But when the believers are called to Allah and His Messenger, in order that he judges between them, their reply is: 'We hear and obey' Such are theprosperous.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The only statement of the [true] believers when they are called to Allah and His Messenger to judge between them is that they say, "We hear and we obey." And those are the successful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When the believers are called to God and His Messenger to be judged, their only words are, "We have listened and obeyed." They will have everlasting happiness.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The response of the believers, when they are invited to Allah and His Apostle that he may judge between them, is only to say: We hear and we obey; and these it is that are the successful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The response of the believers, when they are called to God and His Messenger in order that he may judge between them, is only, "We hear and we obey." It is they who will prosper:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The answer of the Believers, when summoned to Allah and His Messenger, in order that He may judge between them, is no other than this: they say, "We hear and we obey": it is such as these that will attain felicity.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Innama kana qawla almumineena itha duAAoo ila Allahi warasoolihi liyahkuma baynahum an yaqooloo samiAAna waataAAna waolaika humu almuflihoona

52.arabic: وَمَن يُطِعِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَيَخْشَ ٱللَّهَ وَيَتَّقْهِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَآئِزُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Whoever obeys God and His Prophet, fears God and does his duty to Him, will surely find success.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And whoever obeys the command of Allah and His Noble Messenger, and fears Allah, and practices piety – so it is they who are the successful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Whoso obeys God and His Messenger, and fears God and has awe of Him, those -- they are the triumphant.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And whosoever obeyeth Allah and His apostle, and dreadeth Allah and feareth him-these! they are the achievers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And whosoever obeys Allah and His Messenger (SAW), fears Allah, and keeps his duty (to Him), such are the successful ones.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Whoever obeys God and His Messenger, and fears God, and is conscious of Him—these are the winners.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and only those attain true success who obey Allah and His Messenger and fear Allah and refrain from His disobedience.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And whosoever obeys Allah and His Messenger, fears Allah, and has Taqwa of Him, such are the successful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He who obeyeth Allah and His messenger, and feareth Allah, and keepeth duty (unto Him): such indeed are the victorious.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle, and fears Allah and is wary of Him—it is they who will be the triumphant.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who obey Allah and His Messenger, and fear Allah, and have awe of Him, shall be the winners.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And whoever obeys Allah and His Messenger and fears Allah and is conscious of Him - it is those who are the attainers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who obey God and His Messenger, who are humble before Him, and who have fear of Him will, certainly, be successful.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And he who obeys Allah and His Apostle, and fears Allah, and is careful of (his duty to) Him, these it is that are the achievers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: those who obey God and His Messenger, and fear God, and are mindful of their duty to Him, are the ones who will triumph.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: It is such as obey Allah and His Messenger, and fear Allah and do right, that will win (in the end),

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waman yutiAAi Allaha warasoolahu wayakhsha Allaha wayattaqhi faolaika humu alfaizoona

53.arabic: وَأَقْسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ لَئِنْ أَمَرْتَهُمْ لَيَخْرُجُنَّ ۖ قُل لَّا تُقْسِمُوا۟ ۖ طَاعَةٌ مَّعْرُوفَةٌ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They swear solemnly by God: "If you command us we shall go forth." Say: "Do not swear. What is wanted is obedience, as should be. God is certainly aware of what you do."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And they swore by Allah most vehemently in their oaths that if you order them, they will go forth to holy war; say, “Do not swear; obedience according to the law is required; indeed Allah knows what you do.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They have sworn by God the most earnest oaths, if thou commandest them they will go forth. Say: 'Do not swear; honourable obedience is sufficient. Surely God is aware of the things you do.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they swear by Allah with their solemn oaths that, if thou commandest them they will surely go forth. Say thou: swear not, obedience is recognize! verily Allah is Aware of that which ye Work.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They swear by Allah their strongest oaths, that if only you would order them, they would leave (their homes for fighting in Allah's Cause). Say: "Swear you not; (this) obedience (of yours) is known (to be false). Verily, Allah knows well what you do."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they swear by God with their solemn oaths, that if you commanded them, they would mobilize. Say, “Do not swear. Obedience will be recognized. God is experienced with what you do.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They (the hypocrites) solemnly swear by Allah and say, "If you order us, we will leave our homes." Say to them, "Do not swear oaths for your `obedience' is well known; Allah is fully aware of what you are doing. "

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They swear by Allah their strongest oaths that if only you would order them, they would leave. Say: "Swear you not; obedience is known. Verily, Allah knows well what you do."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They swear by Allah solemnly that, if thou order them, they will go forth. Say: Swear not; known obedience (is better). Lo! Allah is Informed of what ye do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They swear by Allah with solemn oaths that if you order them they will surely march out. Say, ‘Do not swear! Honourable obedience [is all that is expected of you]. Allah is indeed well aware of what you do.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They swear by Allah in the most earnest oaths, that if you order them, they would go forth. Say: 'Do not swear, known obedience (is better). Allah is Aware of the things you do'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they swear by Allah their strongest oaths that if you ordered them, they would go forth [in Allah 's cause]. Say, "Do not swear. [Such] obedience is known. Indeed, Allah is Acquainted with that which you do."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They strongly swear by God that they would march to fight (for the cause of God) if you were to order them to. Tell them, "You do not need to swear; fighting for the cause of God is a virtuous deed and God is Well Aware of what you do".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they swear by Allah with the most energetic of their oaths that if you command them they would certainly go forth. Say: Swear not; reasonable obedience (is desired); surely Allah is aware of what you do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They swear firm oaths by God that if you command them to march forth, they will obey you. Say, "Do not swear: your obedience, not your oaths, will count. God is well aware of all your actions."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They swear their strongest oaths by Allah that, if only thou wouldst command them, they would leave (their homes). Say: "Swear ye not; Obedience is (more) reasonable; verily, Allah is well acquainted with all that ye do."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waaqsamoo biAllahi jahda aymanihim lain amartahum layakhrujunna qul la tuqsimoo taAAatun maAAroofatun inna Allaha khabeerun bima taAAmaloona

54.arabic: قُلْ أَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ ۖ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْهِ مَا حُمِّلَ وَعَلَيْكُم مَّا حُمِّلْتُمْ ۖ وَإِن تُطِيعُوهُ تَهْتَدُوا۟ ۚ وَمَا عَلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ إِلَّا ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ٱلْمُبِينُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "Obey God and obey the Apostle. If you turn away, then for him is his duty to fulfil, and for you the burden that you carry; yet if you obey him you will be rightly guided. The duty of the Messenger is to convey the message clearly.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Proclaim, “Obey Allah and obey the Noble Messenger; so if you turn away, then the Noble Messenger is bound only for what is obligatory upon him, and upon you is the duty placed upon you”; and if you obey the Noble Messenger, you will attain guidance; and the Noble Messenger is not liable except to plainly convey.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'Obey God, and obey the Messenger; then, if you turn away, only upon him rests what is laid on him, and upon you rests what is laid on you. If you obey him, you will be guided. It is only for the Messenger to deliver the manifest Message.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: obey Allah and obey the apostle; then if ye turn away, upon him is only that wherewith he hath been laid upon, and upon you is that wherewith ye have been laid upon; if ye obey him, ye will be guided; and naught is upon the apostle except the preaching plain.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say: "Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, but if you turn away, he (Messenger Muhammad SAW) is only responsible for the duty placed on him (i.e. to convey Allah's Message) and you for that placed on you. If you obey him, you shall be on the right guidance. The Messenger's duty is only to convey (the message) in a clear way (i.e. to preach in a plain way)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “Obey God and obey the Messenger.” But if they turn away, then he is responsible for his obligations, and you are responsible for your obligations. And if you obey him, you will be guided. It is only incumbent on the Messenger to deliver the Clarifying Message.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Say, "Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, but if you turn away, you should note it well that the Messenger is responsible for the duty entrusted to him and you are responsible for the duty entrusted to you. If you obey him, you will be rightly guided, for the responsibility of the Messenger is only to convey the Message clearly to you.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, but if you turn away, he is only responsible for the duty placed on him and you for that placed on you. If you obey him, you shall be on the right guidance. The Messenger's duty is only to convey (the Message) in a clear way.") Allah says about the hypocrites who had promised the Messenger and sworn that if he were to command them to go out for battle, they would go:

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: Obey Allah and obey the messenger. But if ye turn away, then (it is) for him (to do) only that wherewith he hath been charged, and for you (to do) only that wherewith ye have been charged. If ye obey him, ye will go aright. But the messenger hath no other charge than to convey (the message) plainly.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Obey Allah, and obey the Apostle.’ But if you turn your backs, [you should know that] he is only responsible for his burden and you are responsible for your own burden, and if you obey him, you will be guided, and the Apostle’s duty is only to communicate in clear terms.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'Obey Allah and obey the Messenger. If you turn away, upon him only rests what is laid upon him, and upon you rests what is laid on you. If you obey him, you shall be guided. It is only for the Messenger to deliver a clear message'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Obey Allah and obey the Messenger; but if you turn away - then upon him is only that [duty] with which he has been charged, and upon you is that with which you have been charged. And if you obey him, you will be [rightly] guided. And there is not upon the Messenger except the [responsibility for] clear notification."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Say to them, "Obey God and His Messenger. If you turn away, the Messenger and the people will each be responsible for their own obligations. If you follow the Messenger, you will have the right guidance. The responsibility of the Messenger is only to preach."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: Obey Allah and obey the Apostle; but if you turn back, then on him rests that which is imposed on him and on you rests that which is imposed on you; and if you obey him, you are on the right way; and nothing rests on the Apostle but clear delivering (of the message).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Obey God and obey the Messenger. If you turn away, then he is responsible for what he is charged with and you are responsible for what you are charged with. If you obey him, you will be rightly guided. The Messenger is responsible only for delivering the message clearly.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "Obey Allah, and obey the Messenger: but if ye turn away, he is only responsible for the duty placed on him and ye for that placed on you. If ye obey him, ye shall be on right guidance. The Messenger's duty is only to preach the clear (Message).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul ateeAAoo Allaha waateeAAoo alrrasoola fain tawallaw fainnama AAalayhi ma hummila waAAalaykum ma hummiltum wain tuteeAAoohu tahtadoo wama AAala alrrasooli illa albalaghu almubeena

55.arabic: وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنكُمْ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ كَمَا ٱسْتَخْلَفَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ وَلَيُمَكِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ دِينَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى ٱرْتَضَىٰ لَهُمْ وَلَيُبَدِّلَنَّهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ خَوْفِهِمْ أَمْنًا ۚ يَعْبُدُونَنِى لَا يُشْرِكُونَ بِى شَيْـًٔا ۚ وَمَن كَفَرَ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: God has promised to make those of you who believe and do the right, leaders in the land, as He had made those before them, and will establish their faith which He has chosen for them and change their fear into security. They will worship Me and not associate any one with Me. But those who disbelieve after this will be reprobates.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Allah has promised those among you who believe and do good deeds, that He will certainly give them the Caliphate in the earth the way He gave to those before them; and that He will certainly establish for them their religion which He has chosen for them, and will turn their prior fear into peace; they must worship Me and not ascribe anything as a partner to Me; and whoever is ungrateful after this – it is they who are the disobedient.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God has promised those of you who believe and do righteous deeds that He will surely make you successors in the land, even as He made those who were before them successors, and that He will surely establish their religion for them that He has approved. for them, and will give them in exchange, after their fear, security: 'They shall serve Me, not associating with Me anything.' Whoso disbelieves after that, those - they are the ungodly.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Allah hath promised those of you who believe and work the righteous works that he shall make them successors on the earth even as He had made those before them successors, and that he shall surely establish for them their religion which He hath approved for them, and that he shall surely exchange unto them after their fear a security, provided they worship Me, associating not aught with Me; and whosoever will disbelieve thereafter, then those! they are the transgressors.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Allah has promised those among you who believe, and do righteous good deeds, that He will certainly grant them succession to (the present rulers) in the earth, as He granted it to those before them, and that He will grant them the authority to practise their religion, that which He has chosen for them (i.e. Islam). And He will surely give them in exchange a safe security after their fear (provided) they (believers) worship Me and do not associate anything (in worship) with Me. But whoever disbelieved after this, they are the Fasiqun (rebellious, disobedient to Allah).

translations.author: Itani
